def: (called yāʼě-ḥut̤t̤ī, 'the yā of the word ḥut̤t̤ī,' or yāʼě-mus̤annāt-ě-taḥtānī, 'the yā with two dots below') is the thirty-fifth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the thirty-second of the Persian, and the twenty-eighth of the Arabic alphabet). It corresponds, as a consonant, to the Devanāgarī य ya, and has the sound of the English y in youth. Like alif and wāw, this letter is also one of the ḥurūf-ě-ʻillat, or 'letters of weakness,' being sometimes used as a vowel, as has been shown at the beginning of the first letter in this volume. In this case, when it helps to produce the vowel sound ī (as in بِين bīn), it is called yāʼě-maʻrūf, 'the known yā'; and when it forms the diphthong e (as in پيڙ peṛ), it is called yāʼě-majhūl, 'the unknown yā.' When used as a number, ي stands for 10; and in almanacs it is the character for Jupiter and the sign Aquarius.
more: called yā-i-huttī or yā-i-musannāt-i-tahtānī , the 28th letter of the Arabic and 32nd of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to य in Sanskrit and y in English. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 10. It is, with ا and و one of the hurūf-i-illat , or weak letters. When preceded immediately by a consonant sounded with kasra it is called yā-i-mārūf (ي). But when preceded by a consonant and sounded with fatah , it is called yā-i-majhūl (ے). Yā-i-nisbatī expresses relation or connexion; as, hindī belonging to India. Yā-i-fāil is affixed to nouns to signify an actor or agent; as, jaṅgī , a warrior, bakvāsī a great talker. Yā-i-mafūl affixed to a noun makes it passive, as dast-khatī signed, mohrī stamped. As a Cont. of H. hī the ī is emphatic; as, isī ko to him in particular. In Hindustāni it is sometimes the termination of diminutive and feminine nouns; as, hāṅḍī, ghoṛī . It is interchangeable as an …

the twenty-eighth letter of the Arabic and thirty-second of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to य in Sanskrit. When appropriated to denote a number, it stands for ten. In almanacks it represents Jupiter, or the sign Aquarius. It is like ا and و one of the ḥurūf-i-iﻌllat, or weak letters, being like the English y sometimes a consonant and sometimes a vowel. In the former case, it is one of the palatine letters, sounding like y in youth. In Sanskrit, the य may be pronounced either like y or j; and in words derived from that language ज is generally substituted for य . When ي sākin or quiescent is preceded immediately by a consonant sounded with kasr, they denote the long vowels ī or e; and, when by one pronounced with fatʼḥa, the dipthong ai. If the first sound is denoted, the ي is called ياي معروف yā-e-maﻌrūf; if the second, ياي مجہول yā-e-majʼhūl; and, if the third, ياي ساکن ماقبل مفتوح …

def: pron. 1˚ corr. of ye, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of yahīṅ, q.v. (called yāʼě-ḥut̤t̤ī, 'the yā of the word ḥut̤t̤ī,' or yāʼě-mus̤annāt-ě-taḥtānī, 'the yā with two dots below') is the thirty-fifth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the thirty-second of the Persian, and the twenty-eighth of the Arabic alphabet). It corresponds, as a consonant, to the Devanāgarī य ya, and has the sound of the English y in youth. Like alif and wāw, this letter is also one of the ḥurūf-ě-ʻillat, or 'letters of weakness,' being sometimes used as a vowel, as has been shown at the beginning of the first letter in this volume. In this case, when it helps to produce the vowel sound ī (as in بِين bīn), it is called yāʼě-maʻrūf, 'the known yā'; and when it forms the diphthong e (as in پيڙ peṛ), it is called yāʼě-majhūl, 'the unknown yā.' When used as a number, ي stands for 10; and in almanacs it is the character for Jupiter and the sign Aquarius.

wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit यवन (yavana), itself from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀬𑁄𑀦 (yona), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎢𐎴 (y-u-n /⁠yauna⁠/). Doublet of یونان (yūnān) and جون (javan).

def: prop. n. Ionia; Greece (so called till subdued by the Romans; since then the term Rūm has been applied to the Eastern Roman empire at large).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یونان (yunān), ultimately from Ancient Greek Ἰωνία (Iōnía, “Ionia”).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Yūnān; Ionian, Grecian, Greek; — an Ionian, a Grecian; — s.f. The Greek language
more: adj. Grecian; Greek. yūnānī tib , The Greek system of medicine.
subs: s.f. The Greek system of medicine.

def: adj. = yūnānī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Yūnān; Ionian, Grecian, Greek; — an Ionian, a Grecian; — s.f. The Greek language
subs: s.f. The Greek system of medicine.

def: s.m. pl. (of Yūnānī), The Greeks.
s.m. Jonah (the prophet).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic يُونُس (yūnus), from Ancient Greek Ἰωνᾶς (Iōnâs), from Hebrew יונה (yônā).

s.m. See the pop. form جوپ jūp.
s.m. Europe
wety: Borrowed from English Europe, ultimately from Ancient Greek Εὐρώπη (Eurṓpē).

def: s.f. Invasion; assault, attack, storm
more: s. m. Assault, storm, invasion. يورش کرنا yūrish karnā, To invade.
subs: To make an attack (on), to invade, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یورش (yūriš), from Chagatai یورش (yöriš).

def: s.m. Europe ( = yūrap).
adj. European.
s.m. See the pop. form جوس jūs.
s.m. The patriarch Joseph; — a proper name.
more: To add, Gen. xxx. 24.) n. prop. The patriarch Joseph.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یُوسُف (yūsuf), borrowed from Arabic يُوسُف (yūsuf), borrowed from Biblical Hebrew יוֹסֵף (yōsép), from הוֹסִיף (hosíf, “to add”).

def: part. (used as a suff.), Attached (to); united, or connected (with), joined (to); in contact (with); accompanied, attended (by); with; possessed (of), furnished (with), endowed (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـِیَّت (-iyyat), in turn from Arabic ـِيَّة (-iyya). Compare Hindi -ता (-tā) and Hindi -त्व (-tva).

def: s.m. corr. of يوتهہ yūth, q.v.
s.m. = yūth, q.v.
s.m. A flock, herd, drove (of birds, animals, &c.), a multitude, number; a body, troop, band, company (see jūth)
subs: vulg. yūthap, or yūth-pati, or yūth-nāth, s.m. Keeper or protector of a troop; lord or captain of a band; leader or head of a flock or herd; — a large elephant (the leader of a wild herd)

def: s.m. See s.v. yūtha or yūth. s.m. A flock, herd, drove (of birds, animals, &c.), a multitude, number; a body, troop, band, company (see jūth)

subs: To throw off the yoke, to refuse the yoke; to be tired (of).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. yuvati, or yuvatī), adj. Young, youthful, juvenile; arrived at puberty, adult, adolescent; — strong, vigorous, healthy; — one who has attained the age of puberty, a young man, a youth (from 16 to 17 years of age; — see the pop. form juvā)
subs: s.m., and yuva-rājya, s.m. See s.v. juvā.

def: adj. = yuvā, q.v. (cf. jawān). s.m. (dialec.) = jūʼā, 'yoke,' q.v. s.m. A yoke; the yoke of a carriage or plough
subs: To throw off the yoke, to refuse the yoke; to be tired (of).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit यवन (yavana), itself from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀬𑁄𑀦 (yona), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎢𐎴 (y-u-n /⁠yauna⁠/). Doublet of یونان (yūnān) and جون (javan).

ety: yuvā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The state or condition of puberty, youth (regarded as a state or condition, or as a time of life), youthfulness, adolescence.
s.m. See juva-rāj, s.v. juva.
s.f. Youthfulness, youth, adolescence.
s.f. A young woman (from 16 to 30 years of age), a damsel (see juvatī).

in compos. for زاده zāda, Born; son.

def: s.m.? Civet (the perfume so called).
more: s. Civet, the perfume so called.

ety: Parsī hizvãn; Pehl. huzvān; Zend hizva; S. जिव्हा
def: s.f. The tongue (syn. jībh); tongue, speech, language, dialect (syn. bolī)
more: n. f. Z. husvān, hozvān ; Pahlvī, zafān ; S. jihvā ; H. jībh . 1. The tongue. 1. Sāre badan meṅ zabān halāl hai . Prov. The tongue is the purest member of the body. 2. Itnī sī jān, gaz bhar kī zabān! Prov. So young, a yard of tongue! 3. Mārte ke hāth pakṛe jāṭe haiṅ, kahte kī zabān nahīṅ pakṛī jātī . Prov. You may hold the hand that strikes, you cannot hold the tongue. 4. Jiskī zabān chale, uske sattar kai chaleṅ . Prov. The power of the tongue is as the power of seventy ploughs. 2. Language ( bolī 3.). zabān baṅd k. , v. a. To hush; silence; shut up; floor. zabān band h. , v. n. 1. To have lockjaw. 2. To be unable to speak. zabān-bandī , n. f. A deposition; a written testimony; an affidavit (in a court of law). zabān-bandī k. , v. a. To take down a deposition. zabān par chaṛhnā , v. n. 1. To be on one's tongue or on the lips. Yeh kyā burā lafz tumhārī zabān par chaṛh gayā hai . 2. To be…

s. f. 1. The tongue. 2. Language, dialect, speech. 3. Flame (of a candle, &c.). زبان آور zabān-āwar, Eloquent, fluent, voluble. زبان آوري zabān-āwar-ī, s. f. Eloquence, fluency, volubility of tongue. زبان بڙهانا zabān baṛhānā, To chatter, to prattle. زبان پرچڙهنا zabān par chaṛhnā, 1. To be publick. 2. To learn by heart. زبان پرلانا zabān par lānā, To speak, say, mention. زبان پکڙنا zabān pakaṛnā, To prevent one from speaking, to criticise. زبان پلٿنا zabān palaṭnā, To equivocate, to retract, to eat oneʼs words. زبان چلاني zabān chalānī, To give abuse. زبان دابنا zabān dābnā, To hold oneʼs tongue. زبان دابکي کـہنا zabān dābke kahnā, To detract in whispers. زبان دان zabān-dān, Skilled in language; a poet, a linguist. زبان داني zabān-dān-ī, s. f. Knowledge of language. زبان دراز zabān-darāz, adj. Abusive. زبان درازي zabān-darāz-ī, s. f. Abuse. زبان درازي کرنا zabān-darāzī karnā, To give ab…
subs: s.f. Eloquence, fluency, volubility of tongue
To let the tongue wag, to chatter, prattle
v.n. To be speechless, be unable to speak; to be stricken dumb; — to have lockjaw
s.f. Written testimony, deposition, affidavit (in a court of law)
v.t. To take down a deposition
(-kī), To obtain the assent, or concurrence, or approval (of)
(-kī), To be on the tongue (of), be talked about, be public; to be on the tip of (one's) tongue, to have or know (a thing) by heart
v.t 'To put on the tongue,' to taste
(-ko), To utter, say, speak of, mention
(-kī), lit. 'To lay hold of the tongue' (of); to prevent (one) from speaking; to interrupt; to seize on the utterances (of), to criticise, to cavil (at)
or zabān phernā, To retract, to eat one's words; to equivocate, to prevaricate, to shuffle
(-kī), The tongue to be exercised in correct articulation; to be able to articulate or pronounce correctly, to speak with correct articulation
(apnī), To talk much, talk at a great rate; to use foul language, to give abuse
To live by a glib use of the tongue, or by flattery
To speak with bated breath; to detract in whispers
adj. & s.m. Skilled in language; — one well-versed in a language; a poet; a scholar; a linguist
s.f. Knowledge of a language, linguistic attainments
adj. 'Long-tongued'; loquacious; speaking much and malevolently; impudent, saucy; abusive, scurrilous
(-ko), To show the tongue (to a physician)
(-meṅ), To interfere by speech, to inquire, ask, request
adj. Talked about, spoken of, reported, public; current, used, in use
(-ke), To be talked about, be currently reported, be often mentioned; to become current (as speech), to be generally used (by), be commonly employed (by), to pass into an idiom
To be careful as to what one says, to speak carefully or cautiously
(apnī), To control (one's) tongue, to be careful as to how one speaks or what one says; to hold one's tongue
v.t. To give utterance to, to utter, pronounce, speak
(-kī), To escape the lips (of); to be uttered, be said

(-kī), To interrupt the speech (of); to make a clicking noise with the (apnī) tongue expressive of regret at a slip or error; to be sorry for what one has said; to grieve, lament; to speak foolishly or inconsiderately
or zabān-ko muṅh-meṅ rakhnā, To keep the tongue under control; to hold one's tongue, to be silent
(apnī), To give loose to the tongue; to speak out
To smack the lips (over), to enjoy or relish; to enjoy dainties
(apnī), To wag the tongue, to speak; to ask, request.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زبان (zabān, zubān), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *hijwáH, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ȷ́iȷ́ʰwáH. First attested in c. 1603 as Middle Hindi زبان (zban /⁠zabān⁠/),. Doublet of جِیبھ (jībh).

def: s.m. Tongue (of fire, or of a buckle, &c.), flame (of a candle).
more: s. m. Flame (particularly, of a candle).

def: adv. By word, orally, &c.
more: adj. 1. By word of mouth; verbal; oral; viva voce . 2. Unwritten; traditional. zabānī imtehān , Oral examination. zabānī paṛhnā , v. a. To read or recite. zabānī jamakharch , Mere words; words, not deeds.
1. s. f. From the tongue, by the mouth, language. 2. adj. Traditional, oral, verbal, by word, of or by or from the tongue.

def: adj. & s.m. Above, upper, high, superior; greater, larger; — the Persian term for the short vowel َ a (so called because it is placed over a letter; — the A. syn. is fatḥa)
more: adj. زبر became large. 1. See bhārī (1-4, 8, 9, 15, 19.). 2. ( zabar-jang ) Big; great; strong. Ghar taṅg, bahū zabar jaṅg . Prov. The but small, the daughter-in-law tall. 3. Higher; above; superior. 4. Greater; larger. zabar, fatah , n. m. The vowel point (') representing a short alif (a) in bal strength. zabar h. , v. n. To have the upper hand.

1. adj. Above, superiour. 2. s. m. The vowel point (َ) pronounced a. زبردست zabar-dast, adj. Vigorous, violent, powerful, victorious, oppressive.زبردستي zabar-dast-ī, 1. s. f. Violence, powerfulness, oppression. 2. adv. Violently, forcibly. زبردستي کرنا zabar dast-ī karnā, To oppress, to force. زبرکرنا zabar karnā, To oppress. زبرهونا zabar honā, To have the upper hand. ¶ زبر zibbir, adj. Powerful, potent.
subs: adj. Having the upper hand, superior, victorious; vigorous, powerful, strong, athletic; high-handed, overbearing, tyrannical, harsh, oppressive, violent
s.f. Superiority; powerfulness; high-handedness, oppression, tyranny, violence, force; — adv. By violence or force, forcibly, violently, wrongfully, unjustly, arbitrarily, wantonly
(-se, or -par), To use violence or force (with or towards), to force, compel; to oppress
v.n. To be superior, to have the upper hand.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَبَر (zabar).

def: adj. 1. Strong ( balvān . 1.); having the upper hand. 2. Stronger. 3. More able; superior. 4. High-handed; violent; oppressive. Zabar-dast māre aur rone na de . Prov. The tyrant smites his subjects down, nor lets his victims moan or groan.
adj. Upper-handed, superiour.

n. f. See bārā-jorī. zabar-dastī, zabar-dastī se , adv. 1. By force or violence; forcibly. 2. Wrongfully; unjustly; per fas et nefas ; arbitrarily. 3. Wantonly. zabar-dastī bhagā le jānā . Forcible abduction. zabar dastī pakaṛ bulānā , v. a. To bring one by force. zabar-dastī chaṛh baiṭhnā , v. a. To commit an indecent assault; to rape. zabar-dastī chhīn lenā, yā le lenā , v. a. To take by force, or snatch from; to extort. zabar-dastī hāzir k . To enforce the production of; to compel one's attendance. zabar-dastī rok rakhnā , v. n. To keep back by force; withhold wrongfully. zabar-dastī izzat lenā , v. a. To make an indecent assault. zabar-dastī qabūl karvānā, yā qubulvānā , v. a. To extort confession. zabar-dastī k. , v. a. 1. To use force; to force; compel; oblige. 2. To press or bear hard upon; to oppress.
wety: From زبردست (zabardast, “superior, powerful”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: zabar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Violence, oppression.

ety: zabar, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Higher, upper; superior.
s.m. A kind of emerald, a chrysolite; a topaz; a beryl; jasper.
more: s. m. A kind of emerald, a chrysolite, a topaz, a beryl, jasper.

ety: act. part. n. of ذبح 'to cut, cleave, slaughter'
def: s.m. Slaughterer, sacrificer; butcher
more: n. m. ذبح cleaving, slaughtering. A butcher.
part. act. (of ذبح) A sacrificer, a butcher. سعد الذابح saﻌdu-ẕ-ẕābiḥ, Two bright stars in the left horn of Capricorn.
subs: The two stars α and β in the left horn of Capricornus; the twenty-second mansion of the moon.

def: s.f. Slaughtering, cutting the throat of, slaying, sacrificing; — ẕibḥ, vulg. ẕibaḥ, s.f. An animal prepared for slaughter, or sacrifice; a sacrifice, a slaughter
more: n. m. A sacrifice; slaughter. zibah k. , Mah. v. a. 1. To slaughter; kill; sacrifice. Mere sāmne bolā to zibah kar ḍalūṅgā . 2. To immolate.

s. f. (Heb. ) A sacrifice, slaughter. ذبح کرنا ẕabḥ karnā, 1. To sacrifice, to immolate. 2. To kill (animals for food agreeably to the Muhammadan law), to slaughter. ذبح هونا ẕabḥ honā, To be sacrificed or killed, &c.
subs: To slaughter, cut the throat of (an animal intended for food, agreeably to the Moḥammadan law); to sacrifice, to immolate; to slay, kill, execute, put to death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِبْح (zibh), from Arabic ذَبْح (ḏabḥ).

def: s.m. The gazelle, the Arabian antelope; a deer, a chevril.
part. adj. Slaughtered, sacrificed; — s.m. An animal that is destined or prepared for sacrifice, an intended victim, a sacrifice ( = ẕabīḥa)
more: (from ذبح) 1. part. Sacrificed. 2. s. m. A sacrifice. ذبيح الله ẕabīḥu-l-lāh, A name of Ismāʼīﻌl, the son of Ibrāhīm.
subs: A surname of Ishmael (who, according to the generality of Moḥammadans, was the son whom Abraham designed to sacrifice, and not Isaac).

ety: for A. ذبيحة ẕabīḥat, fem. of ẕabīḥ
def: s.m. An intended victim, a sacrifice ( = ẕabīḥ); an animal lawfully slaughtered (and fit for food).
more: n. m. An animal lawfully slaughtered and fit for food (Mah).

def: (act. part. of ضبط 'to keep, preserve,' &c.), Careful, cautious, provident, prudent; practising self-control, self-denying, abstemious, continent; — exact, correct, accurate, strict; punctual; — restrainer; possessor, master, ruler, governor
more: adj. 1. Strict; punctual. 2. Self-denying; abstemious; continent. 3. Patient; enduring.
(part. act. of ضبط) s. m. A governour, master, possessor, restrainer. ضابط الکلّ ẓābit̤u-l-kull, Omnipotent (God), lord of all.
subs: Lord or ruler of all; the Omnipotent.

ety: for A. ضابطة, fem. of ẓābit̤, q.v.
def: s.m. Universal rule, general canon, established practice; rule, canon, law, ordinance, regulation; custom; judicial usage; manner of proceeding, procedure
more: s. m. Canon, ordinance, law, rule, regulation. (Plur. ضوابط).
subs: (-kā), To employ legal means, take legal measures; to act according to law
or ḥasb-ě-ẓābit̤a, adv. According to rule or established practice; through the regular channel; in due course
s.m. A body of instructions for revenue officers
adj. and adv. Regular, formal, en règle; — according to rule or established practice; through the regular channel; in due course, duly
adj. Irregular, contrary to rule, informal, unusual.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَابِطَه (zābita), from Arabic ضَابِطَة (ḍābiṭa).

def: s.f. Being according to law or rule; conformity to law or rule, regularity
more: n. f. Conformity to rule or law. be-zābtagī , n. f. Informality; irregularity. be-zābtagī kī sūrat . Want of form. be-zābtagī-i-nālish . Informality of procedure.

ety: inf. n. of ضبط 'to keep, preserve'; 'to withhold, restrain,' &c.
def: s.m. Keeping, taking care of, guarding, defending, watching over, ruling, governing; regulation, government, direction, discipline; restraint, control, check; taking possession of, possession, seizure, confiscation, sequestration, resumption
more: n. m. zabtī , n. f. ضبط held fast. 1. ( zabt ) Control; restraint. 2. The act of confiscating. 3. Seizure; confiscation; resumption; sequestration. zabt k. , v. a. 1. To control; restrain; check (passion). Āh karne men shān jātī hai, Zabt karne meṅ jān jātī hai . One's dignity with sighing goes, One's life in patient suffering goes. 2. To seize or attach property; confiscate; resume (an estate). zabt h., zabtī meṅ ānā , v. n. To be seized, confiscated. zabtī arāzī-i-lā-khirāj yā muāfī . Resumption of rent-free lands. zabtī-i-jāëdād, yā māl . Forfeiture of property. zabtī kā hukm , n. m. An order to confiscate. zabtī ke lāyaq, qābil-i-zabtī , adj. Liable to seizure; contraband.

s. m. Government, direction, discipline, regulation, check, control, possession. ضبط کرنا ẓabt̤ karnā, To take possession, seize, confiscate, rule over, watch, preserve, restrain, control, check. ضبط هونا ẓabt̤ honā, To be taken possession of, to be seized, &c.
subs: v.t. To preserve, watch over, &c.; to rule over; to restrain, control, check; to take possession of, to seize, attach, confiscate, &c.
v.n. To be restrained, be kept under control; — to be taken possession of, be seized, or confiscated, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَبْط (zabt), borrowed from Arabic ضَبْط (ḍabṭ).

def: s.f. Control, restraint; seizure, confiscation, &c. ( = ẓabt̤, q.v.); — adj. Seized, confiscated, &c.
more: 1. s. f. Confiscation, sequestration. 2. adj. Confiscated.
subs: v.n. To come under confiscation, to be seized, or confiscated, &c. ( = ẓabt̤ honā)
adj. Deserving of confiscation; liable to seizure or confiscation; contraband.

ety: v.n. fr. زبن; a post-classical word, with amplification of meaning in Persian
def: adj. Weak, infirm, helpless; vile, evil, ill, bad, wicked, faulty; unfortunate, unlucky; unhealthy (air or climate).
more: adj. Bad, ill, evil, faulty, unlucky, wicked.

def: s.f. Infirmity, weakness, helplessness; vileness, badness, ill, faultiness, wickeduess, vice, depravity; infamy, disgrace.
more: s. f. Vileness, badness, wickedness, vice, faultiness, ill.

def: s.f. The Psalms of David.
more: n. f. زبر written. The Psalms of David.
(from زبر To write) s. f. The psalms of David.

def: s.f. Child-birth (see jaćgī).
s.f. A woman who has recently brought forth (see jaćā).
more: s. f. A lying-in woman: see چچا jachā.

def: s.f. A woman who has recently brought forth (see jaćā).
(dakh.) To grow lean or poor.
n. f. See جچا

ety: v.n. fr. زود 'to lay in a stock of provisions for travelling'
def: s.m. A stock of provisions (for travelling, or for a fixed residence), provisions, food
more: s. m. Food, provisions. زاد راه zād-i-rāh or زاد راحله zād-i-rāḥila, s. m. Way-charges, provisions for travelling.

m. part. pass. (from زادن) used in compos. Born; (substantively) A son, a child; as, آدمي زاد ādmī-zād, A man, born of man; پري زاد parī-zād, Born of an angel or fairy, a beautiful woman; شـﮩزاده shah-zāda, A prince; شـﮩزادي shah-zādī, A princess. زاد بوم zād-būm, Native land, native place.
subs: zād-rāh, and zād-ě-raḥila, s.m. A stock of provisions for travelling; way-charges.

ety: Zend zãtha, rt. zan; S. जातं, rt. जन्; cf. next
def: s.m. Birth, parturition

ety: v.n. of zadan; or Zend jata, rt. jan = S. हतं, rt. हन्
def: s.f. Striking, beating; stroke, blow; damage, loss; — what is struck; object aimed at; range
more: n. f. 1. What is struck; the object of an aim; range. 2. Damage; loss.
subs: s.f. Beating and thumping; assault and battery; fight, conflict (syn. mār-pīṭ).

def: n. f. Assault and battery.

ety: perf. part. of zādan; Pehl. zāt; Zend zāta, rt. zan; S. जात, rt. जन्
def: part. & s.m. (f. zādī), Born, born of; — son, offspring (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ādam-zād, ādmī-zād; parī-zād; shāh-zāda or shah-zāda, shāh-zādī or shah-zādī, &c., qq.v.s.v. ādam, &c.).

r: ẓād, ẓwād, ẓād-ě-mǒʻjama, ẓād-ě-manqūt̤a, z, d
def: (called ẓād-ě-mǒʻjama, or ẓād-ě-manqūt̤a), is the twenty-first letter of the Urdū alphabet (the fifteenth of the Arabic, from which it is taken); — it occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic and the secondary formations therefrom. In Hindūstānī (and Persian) it is pronounced like ز z; but in Arabic it is a strongly articulated palatal d. As a numeral it denotes 800.
more: called ضادمعجمه and ضادمنقوطه ز the 15th letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in Persian, and has no corresponding letter in Sanskrit or Hindi. In Persia and Hindustān its pronounciation is hardly distinguished from that of ز or ذ but in Arabia it is a mixed sound of d , the, and w , only to be acquired from natives of that country. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 800.

the fifteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in Persian, nor is there any corresponding letter in Sanskrit. In Persia and Hindūstān its pronunciation is hardly distinguished from that of ز; but, in Arabia it is a mixed sound of d, th, and w, only to be acquired from natives of that country. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for 800. It is one of the radical letters. The د of Persian words, when they are introduced into Arabic, is often changed into this letter, as ضحاک for ده آک See under ح. When three of these letters meet together, as in تقضّض the darting of a hawk upon his prey, the last is changed into ي, as تقضي. And in verbs of the measure افتعال the servile ت after this letter is changed to ط or to ض as اضطراب or اضّراب from ضرب.

ety: perf. part. of zadan; Pehl. zat; Zend jata, rt. jan; S. हत, rt. हन्
def: part. Struck, stricken, smitten, beaten; affected, afflicted; oppressed; — accentuated; drawn up in line, arrayed (e.g. ṣaf-zada); trimmed, adorned (used in comp., e.g. āfat-zāda, q.v.s.v. āfat); — old, worn out, ragged (as clothes, &c.).
more: p. p. Used in comp. only. 1. Struck; beaten; smitten. 2. Affected; oppressed; afflicted, as musībat-zada . 3. Drawn up in a line; arrayed. Saf-zadah .
adj. 1. See خراب 1, 2, 3. 2. Old; used up; worn out. Yeh kapṛā to bilkul zadah hai .

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَایِع (zāyi'), borrowed from Arabic ضَائِع (ḍāʔiʕ), from ضَاعَ (ḍāʕa). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi ضایع (ẓay'). Compare Punjabi ضائَع (ẓāyaʻ) / ਜ਼ਾਇਆ (zāiā).

ety: zāʼe, fr. zādan, rt. Zend zan = S. जन्+dim. aff. ća = S. क

def: n. m. ذوق tasted. 1. The sense of tasting; the palate. 2. Flavour; savour; smack ( chaskā , 1.). zāëqā lenā , v. a. To taste; relish; smack one's chops. zāëqe-dār , adj. Tasty; savoury; well-flavored.
s.m. Quickness of death; sudden death.

ety: inf. n. of ظفر 'to be successful'; 'to be victorious,' &c.
def: s.m. (f.?) Victory, triumph; success; profit, gain
more: n. f. 1. Victory; triumph. 2. Gain; profit. Safar wasīlaë zafar . Prov. Travel is gain.
s. m. Victory. ظفرآيات z̤afar-āyāt, adj. Victory (in) signs, victorious. ظفر ياب z̤afar-yāb, Victorious, conqueror.
subs: adj. Having the signs of victory, victorious
s.m. A long, sharp, iron prong; a dirk, stiletto
s.m. A congratulatory letter on the occasion of a victory
adj. & s.m. Victorious, triumphant; — a conqueror.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ظفر (zafar), from Arabic ظَفَر (ẓafar).

def: s.f. Saffron (cf. Ital. zafferano).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَعْفَرَان (za'farān), from Arabic زَعْفَرَان (zaʕfarān).

more: 1. adj. Of saffron, saffroncoloured, yellow. 2. s. f. A yellow colour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَعْفَرَانِی (za'farānī), from Arabic زَعْفَرَانِيّ (zaʕfarāniyy). By surface analysis, زَعْفْرَان (za'frān) + ـِی (-ī).

ety: prob. a corr. of A. صفير, inf. n. of صفر 'to whistle'; cf. P. sapīl

more: n. f. A loud whistle; a bird-call. zafīl denā , v. a. (Pigeon-fliers) To whistle ( sīṭī bajānā ).
s. (dakh.) A whistling (loud), a bird call ( Harris ).

def: v.n. To whistle.

ety: prob. a corr. of A. صفير, inf. n. of صفر 'to whistle'; cf. P. sapīl
def: s.f. Whistling; a loud whistle (such as is used in making pigeons fly)
s.f. A whistle.
more: n. f. زفر drawing in of the breath. A whistle. Karte haiṅ āvāz, zafīrī dete haiṅ, dastak sau sau bār, Ghar meṅ patthar pheṅkte hain, zanjīr-i-dar khaṭkāte haiṅ . Momin. Now noises made, now whistles used, palms struck a hundred times, Now stones thrown in the house, and now the door chain shook sometimes. (The lover seeking a meeting with his mistress).
s. (dakh.) A sort of wind musical instrument ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. A crow
more: s. m. A crow. زاغ آبي zāg̠ẖ-i-ābī, A coot.

more: n. f. Cor. of P. zagand . 1. A spring; bound. 2. A kite ( chīl ). zaqan lagānā, yā mārnā , v. a. To leap; bound.

ety: inf. n. of ذهب 'to go, depart,' &c.

s.m. Gold; — the yolk of an egg.

ety: act. part. of زهد 'to abstain from'
def: adj. & s.m. Abstinent, religious, devout; — one who shuns the world and exercises himself in acts of devotion, a devotee, a monk, recluse, hermit; a zealot.
more: (part. act. of زهد) 1. s. m. A monk, a hermit, a recluse, a zealot. زاهدا zāhid-ā, O zealot! 2. adj. Religious, devout. (Pers. plur. زاهدان zāhidān ).

def: n. m. زﮩد abstained. A devout man; a monk. Zāhid-i-gumrāh ke kis tarah maiṅ hamrāh hūṅ, Woh kahe 'Allah hū', aur maiṅ kahūṅ 'Allah hūṅ!' Zauq. How can I company keep with this lost devotee, He cries 'God is,' and I cry, 'God exists in me!'

def: adv. In the manner of a zāhid, like a zāhid, devoutly.

ety: zāhid, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Abstinence, continence, devotion; the life of a devotee or recluse.
more: s. f. The life of a recluse, devotion.

def: (act. part. of ضحك 'to laugh'), Laugher (at), mocker, ridiculer, satirist.
more: (part. act. of ضحک Heb. and Ch. and To laugh) 1. Satyrist, mocker, ridiculer. 2. Name of a poet.

def: adj. Acute (ذکي).

def: adj. & s.m. Outward, exterior, external, extrinsic, exoteric; appearing, apparent, overt, open, perceptible, visible, perceived, plain, evident, manifest, conspicuous, ostensible; — the outside, the external appearance; the external, or outward, or extrinsic state, or condition, or circumstances (of, -kā); the outward, or apparent, character, or disposition of the mind
more: (part. act. of ظﮩر Heb. To shine, to appear) adj. Apparent, evident, open, manifest. s. m. The outside, the external, appearance. ظاهر پرست z̤āhir-parast, adj. Minding the outside or external; a superficial observer. ظاهر دار z̤āhir-dār, adj. Specious, plausible, showy. ظاهر کرنا z̤āhir karnā, To publish, show, disclose, discover, reveal, exhibit, manifest, explain, tell, pretend. ظاهر مين z̤āhir meṅ, Openly, apparently, in publick. ظاهر نما z̤āhir-numā, adj. Specious, plausible. ظاهر هونا z̤āhir-honā, v. n. To become publick or manifest, to be revealed, to be disclosed, to appear; the revelation.
subs: s.m. One who looks only to the exterior
adj. & s.m. Minding the outside or exterior, &c.; — a superficial observer; one who is influenced by outward show; one who takes things for what they seem
s.m. The head or chief of the caste of ḥalāl-ḵẖor, q.v.
adj. Specious, plausible, showy, ostentatious; pretentious; ceremonious, formal
s.f. Speciousness; show, ostentation; ceremony, formality; — z̤āhir-dārī baratnā (-kī), To make a show (of), to affect, pretend, to assume; to pass off (for)
v.t. To make evident or plain (to, -se), to show, exhibit, manifest, tell, explain, disclose, discover, reveal; to publish; to expose; — to make a show of, to make a pretence of, to pretend, affect
adv. Openly; apparently, in public; in appearance, ostensibly
s.m. Outward appearance and inward qualities; — adv. Outwardly and inwardly; inside and out

or ho-jānā, v.n. To be or become apparent or visible; to become manifest (from, -se), to be clearly shown (by), to appear, to become public, to become evident or known (to, -par), to be disclosed, be divulged, be revealed.
wety: From Classical Persian ظاهر (zāhir), from Arabic ظَاهِر (ẓāhir).

def: s.m. Aider, assistant, associate, ally; — a poetical name of a Persian poet of Faryāb.

adj. ظﮩر was apparent. See parghat . 1. Palpable; plain; evident. Zāhir ābād, bātin kharāb . Prov. Saying. Fair without and foul within. 2. Patent; prominent; glaring. zāhir-parast , n. m. One who takes things for what they seem; one who is influenced by outward shows. Dunyā zāhir-parast hai . Prov. The whole world wide but worshippers of what's outside. zāhirdār' , adj. 1. Showy; ostentatious. 2. Pretentious. 3. Ceremonious; formal. zāhir-dārī , n. f. 1. Show; ostentation. 2. Formality; ceremony. zāhir-dārī baratnā , v. a. To affect; make a show; pretend to; assume; pass off for. zāhir k. , v. a. See prakāsh k . 1. To publish ( ishtehār denā , 1—3). 2. To reveal; disclose; expose ( ifshā k .). 3. To explain; make plain. zāhir o bātin . Ped. for H. bāhar bhītar , q. v. Outwardly and inwardly; inside and out. Āp kā zāhir kuchh hai, aur bātin kuchh. zāhir ho ki . Be it known that; whereas; seeing that. zāhir h. , v. n. 1. To be visible or apparent. 2. To become known, etc. zāhir hai It is obvious. iflās zāhir k. , v. a. To sham poverty. razā-mandī zāhir k. , v. a. To signify consent. lā-ilmī zāhir k. , v. a. To affect ignorance; to pretend not to know.
(from ظﮩر) s. m. 1. An assistant, associate, ally. 2. A poetical name of a Persian poet.
= P ظاهره z̤āhira (for A. ظاهرة, fem. of z̤āhir), adj. f. = z̤āhir, q.v.

adv. Apparently; so far as appearances go; ostensibly ( dekhat meṅ ). zāhirā sūrat-i-maāsh . Ostensible means of subsistence.
adv. Outwardly, openly, publicly, manifestly, evidently, plainly, apparently, to all appearance, seemingly, ostensibly.

adj. Apparent; ostensible (اوپری 1—3). zāhirī hālat . External state or condition.

Poisonous. زهرقاتل zahr-i-ḳātil, Deadly poison. زهرهلاهل zahr-i-halāhal (comp. of زهر and هلاهل or Sans. हलाहल Mortal venom) s. m. Deadly poison. زهرکهاناکسي پر zahr khānā kisī par, To imitate an act unsuitable to oneʼs state or condition, to ape. زهرمارکرنا zahr-i-mār karnā, To do a thing by force or unwillingly. زهرمـہره zahr-muhra, s. m. The bezoar, an antidote to poison.
subs: lit. 'To vomit poison'; to say venomous, spiteful, or bitter things; to speak ill (of), to calumniate; to sneer (at)
and zahr-āmez, adj. Mixed with poison, poisoned, envenomed, venomous
s.m. lit. 'Poison-air'; a dangerous disease of horses and elephants in which the yard and testicles of the animal become suddenly swollen and violently inflamed

v.n. To swallow or take poison (on account of, or through love, &c., for anyone, -kisī-par); to be exceedingly envious or jealous (of anyone, -kisī-par); to imitate an act unsuitable to one's state or condition, to ape
v.t. To give (one) poison, to poison
(-ko), To be regarded as poison, to be hateful (to)
v.n. To swallow as poison, to do (a thing) by force, or with great repugnance
s.m. Bezoar, an antidote to poison
wety: From Classical Persian زَهْر (zahr).

def: n. m. 1. Poison (بس). 2. ( zahr-bād ) The bitter principle. zahr , adj. 1. Bitter; bitter as gall. 2. Pernicious. 3. Hateful; abominable; odious; obnoxious. Zahr kar detī hai woh khāne ko laṛ-ke mujh se roz, Āj se maiṅ sāth uske khānā khāūṅ dūr pār! Wom. Rangīn. My meals to poison she just turns, she fights with me each day, Out on it if a morsel I do touch e'en from today! zahr ugalnā , v. n. See bis ulagnā. zahr-ālūdah , adj. Impregnated with poison; poisonous. zahr-bād , n. m. A dangerous disease in horses and elephants in which the yard and testicles of the animal become suddenly swollen and violently inflamed. zahr-bād chaṛhnā , v. n. To circulate in the body (poison). zahr-dār , adj. Poisonous. zahr-i-qātil, zahr-i-halāhal , A deadly poison. zahr khānā , v. n. To take poison ( bis khānā ). zahr khānā kisī par . To take poison on account of one (through love, jealousy, shame, etc.); to perform the happy despatch. zahr kī se ghūṅṭ pīnā , v. n. lit . to gulp down as poison. To suppress one's feelings; to bear patiently. zahr lagnā , v. n. To be hateful like poison. zahr-mār ; A. tiryāq , adj. Poison-destroying. zahrmär k. , v. n. To swallow as poison. Do chār nivāle zahrmār kar-ke khaṛā ho gayā .

def: (for A. ضعيفة, fem. of ẓaʻīf), adj. fem. = ẓaʻīf, q.v. adj. Weak, feeble, impotent, frail, infirm, emaciated, old; faint, low; unsound; insignificant; poor, wretched, distressed
subs: adj. Of weak faith or belief; credulous
adj. Frail of structure or body
adj. Emaciated in body, low in flesh; insignificant in size or body
adj. In wretched circumstanees, in distress
adj. Of weak or unsound mind, weak-minded
v.n. To be or become weak or infirm; to become old.

ety: ẓaʻīf, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Weakness, infirmity, emaciation; old age; exhaustion.
adj. & s.m. Biting; — a biter; a lion.
more: part. Biting. s. m. A lion.

ety: act. part. n. of ذاق (for ذوق 'to taste'
def: adj. & s.m. Tasting; — one who tastes, taster.
part. act. (of ذوق) Tasting.
(from ذوق) s. m. The sense of tasting, the palate, relish, taste. ذايقه شناس ẕāʼịka-shinās, Knowing in taste. ذايقه لينا ẕāʼịḳa lenā, To taste.

ety: for A. ذائِقة ẕāʼiqat; see ẕāʼiq
def: s.m. The sense of tasting; the palate; taste, relish, flavour, savour, smack
subs: vulg. ẕāʼiqe-dār, adj. Possessing relish, tasty, savoury
adj. & s.m. Knowing in taste; — one who is skilled in taste
(-kā), To have a taste (of), to taste, to relish, smack the lips (over).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذَائِقَه (zā'iqa), from Arabic ذَائِقَة (ḏāʔiqa). Related to ذوق (zauq).

def: s.m. The latter, hinder, last, or hindmost part (of anything), lower part, bottom; supplementary part; skirt, or lower extremity (of a garment), train (of a robe); train, retinue; sequel, that which follows, or is annexed
more: s. m. The skirt of a garment, the train of a robe; the last, hinder, or supplementary part of any thing; train, retinue, the sequel, that which follows or is annexed, a list of particulars mentioned in a letter, an appendix.
subs: adv. As below, as follows, as under-mentioned

ety: act. part. of زول 'to pass away, to cease,' &c.
def: part. adj. Passing away, ceasing to be or exist, failing, perishing, vanishing, waning, declining; transient; deficient; nonexistent
subs: v.n. To pass away, to cease, fail, &c.

def: (part. act. of زوال) adj. Deficient, failing, perishing, vanishing, waning, declining.
adv. ذيل had a long tail or pendant. As follows; as undermentioned; as proceeding; as below. hasb-i-mufassila zail . As detailed below.
s.m. Adorning, decking; an ornament, a grace, a beauty.

n. A supposed demon. Kyā tere sir ā chaṛhe chāroṅ ke chāroṅ, alamā! Shāh Daryā, Shaikh Saddo, Zain Khāṅ, Nanne Miyāṅ? Wom. Inshā. What! on your head four demons all alighted, every one, Shekh Saddo, Shāh Daryā, Zain Khān, and Nanneh Miyān beknown!
part. & s.m. Visiting; — visitor, visitant; pilgrim.
(part. act. of زور) s. m. A visitant; a pilgrim.
s.m. The olive tree; an olive.
wety: From Arabic زَيْتُون (zaytūn) (through Classical Persian زیتون (zaytūn)).

def: adj. Narrow, strait; contracted; illiberal, niggardly, avaricious; — s.m. A niggard, miser, narrow-hearted man.
(part. act. of ضيع) adj. Lost, destroyed, perished, fruitless, abortive. ضايع کرنا ẓāʼịﻌ ḳarnā, To lose, to destroy, to consume.

ety: orig. zāk or zāg; prob. = S. कृष्णं, cf. zang; A. zāj, fr. the P.
def: s.m. Blue or green vitriol, copperas; black, blacking; alum
more: s. m. Copperas, vitriol. زاج سفيد zāj-i-sufaid, s. m. Alum.

def: s.m. Chiding, checking, forbidding roughly or harshly, threatening, threat, a cry by which one chides; an impediment.
adj. Distressed (in mind), disquieted, vexed, grieved; melancholy; peevish, fretful.

ety: said to be fr. the (prob. post-classical
def: s.f. Defeat, repulse, check; injuring, injury, damage, loss; disgrace, humiliation, insult, affront; — deceiving, baulking, disappointing
more: n. f. زک totter from weakness. 1. Defeat; repulse. 2. Affront; insult; humiliation. 3. Loss; damage; injury. zak uṭhānā, yā pānā , v. n. To suffer humiliation or disgrace. Ab dil ko lagāte haiṅ zarā soch samajh-ke, Do chār jagah pahle jo zak pāë hue haiṅ . Sābir. 2. To suffer loss or damage. zak denā , v. a. 1. To defeat; beat. 2. To put to shame; affront. 3. To cause loss or injury.
s. f. Injuring, deceiving, disappointing. زک اٿهانا zak uṭhānā, To be ashamed. زک دينا zak denā, To put to shame.
subs: v.n. To suffer injury, or loss, or damage; to suffer humiliation or disgrace, to experience shame, be put to shame
(-ko), To beat, defeat, check; to affront; to humiliate, put to shame; to cause injury or loss (to).

ety: inf. n. of ذکي, for ذکو 'to be sharp or acute in mind'
def: s.m. Sharpness or acuteness of mind, quickness of perception or understanding, acumen, sagacity, penetration.
more: (from ذکي To blaze, to be acute in mind. Heb. To be pure, clean, splendid) s. m. Acumen, acuteness of genius, penetration, sagacity. ¶ ẕukā, s. m. The sun.

def: s.m. The chin; beard
more: n. m. 1. Chin. 2. (Cor, of P. zagand ) Bound; spring. chāh-i-zaqan , Poet, n. m. A dimple in the chin.
(Heb. The beard) s. m. The chin, the beard. چاه ذقن chāh-i-ẕaḳan, s. m. A dimple in the chin.

ety: corr. of P. زغند ẕag̠and, q.v.
def: s.m. Spring, bound.
adj. & s.m. Male, of the male sex, masculine; — a male; — the male organ of generation, membrum virile, penis.
more: s. m. Membrum virile, male, masculine.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذکر (zakar), from Arabic ذَكَر (ḏakar).

ety: v.n. fr. زکو 'to receive increase and blessing from God'
def: s.f. Alms, poor-rate, the portion or amount of his property that is given by a Musalman (agreeably to the rules laid down in the Qorʼān) to the poor.
more: (from زکا) s. f. Alms, a portion of a Musalmānʼs property given in charity agreeably to the rules laid down in the ḳurʼān.

def: s.f. = زکاة zakāt, q.v. s.f. Alms, poor-rate, the portion or amount of his property that is given by a Musalman (agreeably to the rules laid down in the Qorʼān) to the poor.
more: n. f. زکو blessed by God. 1. Alms. 2. A portion of a Mussalman's property given in charity agreeably to the Qurān .

ety: for A. ذکاوة, abst. n. fr. ذکو 'to be sharp,' &c.; prob. a post-classical word
def: s.f. Brightness of genius, wit, acuteness, &c. ( = ẕakā, q.v.). s.m. Sharpness or acuteness of mind, quickness of perception or understanding, acumen, sagacity, penetration.
more: n. f. ذکو was sharp. Acuteness; penetration; acumen.
s. f. Brightness of genius, acuteness, acumen, ingenuity.

ety: for A. زکاوة, v.n. fr. زکم 'to be or become pure'
def: s.f. Purity; innocence; probity; ingenuity.
more: (from زکا) s. f. Probity, purity, ingenuity.

def: s.f. pl. (of zakāt), Alms.
s.m. contrac. of زخم zaḵẖm, q.v.
s. plur. (of ذخيره) Victuals, provision, treasures, stores.
